description: Keep cryptographic primitives, general secure hardware, and broad identity/authentication systems independent of the later X.509 PKI architecture.

checks.reason: Digital-signature constructions predate X.509 and are cryptographic primitives used by PKI, not products of it.
HSMs provide general key protection and cryptographic processing for PKI and non-PKI applications alike.
Digital identity can use passwords, shared secrets, OTP seeds, biometrics, or asymmetric keys without a certificate authority.
Enterprise identity and access management supports shared-secret, OTP, federation, and certificate-based architectures.
MFA is defined by combining factors and can use passwords, OTP, out-of-band, or biometric methods without X.509.
